history: Presented with neck and shoulder pain
presentation: Intradural, intramedullary mass of the cervical spinal cord at levels C2–C6
Mass of 6.4 cm length and 1.7 cm diameter
Inhomogeneous, hyperintense lesion on T2 weighted MRI
Consecutive syringomyelia ranging from C6/7 to T1/2
Iso- to hypointense lesion on T1 weighted imaging with mild to moderate, inhomogeneous contrast enhancement
diagnostics: Histological evaluation showed pleomorphic glial tumor cells, mitoses, calcifications, and atypical ganglioid cells
Immunohistochemistry showed tumor cells positive for GFAP, retained ATRX expression, no expression of IDH1 R132H mutant protein
Histone H3.3 K27M mutant protein strongly expressed in the nuclei of tumor cells
Molecular workup revealed H3F3A K27M, TERT C228T, and PDGFRα Y849C mutations
Epigenome-wide methylation profiling confirmed the methylation class of diffuse midline glioma
management: Concomitant radiochemotherapy with Temozolomide analogous to STUPP protocol
Local application of 28 × 1.8 Gy including residual tumor volume and high risk areas
outcome: Postoperative MRI showed partial resection with minimal residual contrast enhancement
Incomplete cross-sectional sensorimotor syndrome from C4 that gradually declined under physical therapy
